description: learn:execute embeds JSON inside single quotes without escaping when calling learn:update-progress and learn:write-profile.
proposed_fix: Create a shared escaping helper (single-quote safe) or switch to file-based payloads (write JSON to temp file and pass a path). Quote all interpolated args.
title: Fix JSON shell-escaping for learn:execute CLI calls (evidence + profile writes)

description: mcp-runner runs without Node permission flags on Node < 22; preload is not a complete sandbox for untrusted code.
proposed_fix: If Node < 22, refuse to run untrusted code verification or run in a stronger sandbox boundary. Add explicit messaging and tests.
title: Fail closed or strengthen sandbox for mcp-runner on Node < 22

description: learn:plan uses mv via Bash() and unquoted paths; learn:profile uses ls globbing. These commands can fail on Windows or different shells.
proposed_fix: Use CLI endpoints (learn:list-profiles, future learn:write-session) or use Read/Write for file operations in a portable way. Quote paths.
title: Replace mv/cat/ls bash-only usages in learn:plan and learn:profile for cross-platform reliability

description: A crash can leave .workflow/learn/.lock behind and block all learn commands.
proposed_fix: Implement stale lock detection (pid not alive and/or created_at older than threshold) and auto-clean. Optionally add explicit learn:unlock command with guard rails.
title: Add stale-lock recovery (or learn:unlock) to prevent permanent LOCKED state

description: learn:update-state accepts arbitrary strings for active_profile_id and active_session_id; schema also allows arbitrary strings.
proposed_fix: Validate ids using PROFILE_ID_RE and SESSION_ID_RE in learn:update-state; optionally tighten schema with pattern constraints.
title: Validate active_profile_id and active_session_id in learn:update-state

description: learn:plan writes session files directly with Write()/Bash(), bypassing withLearnLock and atomicWriteJson safeguards.
proposed_fix: Add ccw learn:create-session / learn:write-plan / learn:write-progress endpoints using withLearnLock + schema validation, and have learn:plan call them.
title: Stop bypassing lock-protected CLI APIs in learn:plan (add session write endpoints)

description: learn:execute computes phases for legacy plans in memory but does not persist.
proposed_fix: Persist phases to plan.json once (atomic + lock) via CLI.
title: Persist phase assignment upgrades to plan.json to avoid repeated legacy upgrade

description: There are no automated tests for learn state commands or for command-doc helpers.
proposed_fix: Add tests for: withLearnLock (including stale lock), atomicWriteJson, schema failures, safeExecJson/lastJsonObjectFromText, JSON escaping round-trips.
title: Add test coverage for learn state CLI and helpers (lock, schema, escaping)

description: LOCKED errors do not guide users toward recovery.
proposed_fix: Include lockInfo (pid, created_at) in message and add guidance (wait, confirm process, unlock).
title: Improve LOCKED UX: include pid/age and suggested recovery steps

description: Progress is written without validation, risking drift.
proposed_fix: Define learn-progress.schema.json and validate in learn:update-progress; add migrations if needed.
title: Add schema validation for progress.json (learn-progress.schema.json)

description: Logger reads entire log then writes back with append; slow and race-prone.
proposed_fix: If available, use append; otherwise keep bounded logs or write per-entry file and aggregate.
title: Replace logger read+overwrite with append-friendly strategy
